title: albizia vialeana
intro: Albizia vialeana, also known as the Indian siris or white siris, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Asia, particularly in India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Albizia vialeana lie in its bark, leaves, and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin ailments. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as prevent infections. Herbal preparations of Albizia vialeana include a decoction of the bark, which is used to treat fever, and a paste made from the leaves, which is applied topically to treat skin conditions.
